# YouTube Operations (Nana + Tutor)

## Channel Positioning

Official tutorials + Product showcase + Customer stories — three pillars.

**Core Goals**: Accelerate store setup, reduce training costs, build brand trust, SEO + lead generation.

## Nana — Channel Operations

### Full Pipeline

| Area | Responsibility |
|------|---------------|
| **Topic Planning** | Content calendar based on competitive analysis, trends, user demand |
| **Channel Analysis** | Competitor teardowns (structure, cadence, engagement); own channel data review |
| **Channel Config** | Homepage layout, playlists, About/Banner/Logo, keywords, default upload settings |
| **Upload & Optimization** | Title/description/tag SEO, thumbnails, End Screen/Cards, subtitles/CC, timing, Shorts |
| **Data Analysis** | YouTube Analytics (CTR, AVD, retention, traffic sources); monthly/weekly reports; growth tuning |

### Skills

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **youtube-plan-new-video** | Full planning workflow with A/B test thumbnails |
| **youtube-seo** | Title (<60 chars), description, tags, thumbnail strategy, Google/AI Overviews optimization |

### Past Experience

Managed YouTube channels for Square POS and Toast POS. Deep familiarity with North American B2B restaurant SaaS content strategies and audience profiles.

## Tutor — Tutorial Video Production

### Target Audiences

| Audience | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| ISO Agent Partners | Installation training |
| Internal New Employees | Onboarding training |
| Potential Restaurant Owners | Product understanding and decisions |

### Video Classification (5 Categories, 20 Topics)

#### Category 1: Get to Know Peblla (Brand Awareness)
1. Company Introduction
2. Hardware Unboxing
3. Software Tour

#### Category 2: Hardware Installation (P0 — Highest Priority)
4. POS Terminal Setup
5. KDS Installation
6. Printer Setup
7. Network Config
8. Card Reader Setup

#### Category 3: Software Tutorials
9. Menu Setup
10. Order Management
11. Online Ordering
12. Reports & Analytics
13. Employee Management

#### Category 4: Peblla Solutions
14. Sushi
15. Conveyor Belt Sushi
16. Ramen
17. Boba Tea

#### Category 5: Restaurant Stories
18. Customer Story
19. Troubleshooting
20. Complete Installation Walkthrough

### Production Priority

| Priority | Topics |
|----------|--------|
| **P0** | #4 POS Terminal Setup, #5 KDS Installation, #20 Complete Walkthrough |
| **P1** | #6 Printer Setup, #7 Network Config, #8 Card Reader Setup |
| **P2** | #1 Company Intro, #9 Menu Setup, #10 Order Management |
| **P3** | All remaining |

### Video Format Standards

- Unified intro/outro
- 16:9 landscape, 1080p minimum
- Screen recording + live footage combination
- Key steps annotated with text overlays

### Publishing Cadence

| Phase | Frequency |
|-------|-----------|
| Initial | 1-2 per week (P0 first) |
| Mid-term | 1 per week |
| Long-term | 1 per two weeks |

### Distribution Strategy

- Embed in Help Center
- Cut 60s highlight for TikTok/Reels
- Create PDF Quick Start Guides
- Include video links in onboarding emails

### Competitor References

| Brand | Style |
|-------|-------|
| Otter POS | Clean how-to |
| Square POS | Standardized hardware installation |
| Toast POS | Role-based modular training |
| SpotOn | Feature-modular topics |

## Training Video Decisions (Meeting 2026-03-17)

- **Target**: Internal installation staff (not customer-facing)
- **Method**: On-site filming + AI re-editing (swap backgrounds/characters)
- **Structure**: One product per video
- **Format options**: Video + card-style carousel (one step per card, click to advance)
- **Direction**: Lean into AI-assisted production
- **Meeting notes**: https://riverroad.feishu.cn/docx/BL7OdCq3boB3GYxprHqcM4R7n2f
